steps.title: Identify the key signal for maladjustment
steps.content: The passage claims the professor cannot adjust to the estate. The strongest signal is the professor contrasting what he was accustomed to with how he feels at the estate.

steps.title: Match the quotation to the claim
steps.content:
Choice D shows the professor contrasting being “used to” his library, lecture hall, and colleagues' esteem with being “plunged into this wilderness,” “condemned” to witness “stupid people” and “futile conversations.” The words “condemned” and his contemptuous tone directly express his inability to accept country life.

The others lack the professor's own voice expressing explicit discontent with the estate.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
